commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Crownest delegated-run provider with CLI config/env handling, provider registration, docs, tests, and a live smoke script.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new provider PR, not a bug report. The PR body includes live Crownest doctor/run/smoke output that exercises the proposed behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer provider/product acceptance, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: [codex] Add Crownest provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Crownest delegated-run provider with archive sync, Workspace Run execution, provider docs, matrix/category updates, config/env handling, and a live smoke script.
- Hardens Crownest lifecycle behavior around claim cleanup, failed setup, cancellation, kept sandboxes, status waits, cleanup command scoping, stream failures, per-lease operation locking, and redaction of presigned upload errors.
- Adds focused Go coverage for provider/client/CLI behavior plus script coverage for live smoke classification.

## Why / impact

This lets Crabbox run workloads on Crownest while keeping the core provider-neutral and avoiding secret persistence or secret forwarding. The provider treats the Crownest API key as runtime-only configuration, does not pass it on the command line, rejects unsupported delegated-run surfaces, and documents the current environment-forwarding limitation.

## Review comment addressed

- Removed unrelated `worker/package-lock.json` drift from the branch.
- Added Crownest per-lease operation locking for retained run reuse, stop, and cleanup paths so concurrent local processes cannot race over the same retained sandbox.
- Made retained-session cleanup commands carry the Crownest selector scope (`--crownest-url`, `--crownest-project-id`, `--crownest-template`) so custom-scoped retained sandboxes can be stopped from the printed command.
- Addressed Codex review on one-shot cleanup: when Crownest owns cleanup with `keepSandbox=false`, Crabbox removes only the local claim and does not call `DeleteSandbox`; retained sandboxes still use explicit delete when Crabbox requested retention and later cleans up.

## Repo guidance checked

- `AGENTS.md`: provider-neutral core, no generated outputs edited by hand, verification commands included in the PR, and no provider/broker tokens in code/docs.
- `SECURITY.md`: no live credentials or private infrastructure details are published here.
- `.github/`: no pull request template is present; CI target is the repository default branch, `main`.
- Crownest API docs referenced for provider integration context: https://docs.crownest.dev/docs

## Validation

- `go test ./internal/providers/crownest ./internal/cli`  
  Passed.
- `go vet ./...`  
  Passed.
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`  
  Passed. macOS linker emitted only: `ld: warning: ignoring duplicate libraries: '-lobjc'`.
- `scripts/check-docs.sh`  
  Passed: checked 51 command docs, provider matrix with 68 providers, 201 markdown files, and built `dist/docs-site`.
- `node --test scripts/live-crownest-smoke.test.js`  
  Passed: 1 test.
- `git diff --exit-code origin/main -- worker/package-lock.json`  
  Passed: worker lockfile is no longer part of this PR.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`  
  Clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported.

### Live Crownest evidence

Using a Crownest API key from the shell environment only, I ran `bin/crabbox doctor --provider crownest` and a real remote workload against a temporary repo containing six files. The remote command ran Node tests, analyzed a CSV dataset, wrote a summary artifact, and verified the aggregate output.

Key output:

```text
ok      provider provider=crownest timeout=10s auth=ready api=ready mutation=false runtime=ready
sync candidate: 6 files, 8.7 KiB
leased [redacted] slug=[redacted] provider=crownest sandbox=[redacted]
v22.22.3
# pass 3
analysis complete {"totalEvents":240,"totalFailures":34,"totalCostCents":6582}
verification complete {"totalEvents":240,"totalFailures":34,"totalCostCents":6582,"teams":[{"team":"alpha","count":60,"failures":8,"failureRate":0.1333,"p95DurationMs":851,"totalCostCents":1680},{"team":"beta","count":60,"failures":9,"failureRate":0.15,"p95DurationMs":838,"totalCostCents":1606},{"team":"delta","count":60,"failures":8,"failureRate":0.1333,"p95DurationMs":820,"totalCostCents":1628},{"team":"gamma","count":60,"failures":9,"failureRate":0.15,"p95DurationMs":783,"totalCostCents":1668}]}
crownest run summary sync=58ms command=4.898s total=17.073s exit=0
```

I also ran the live smoke script with `CRABBOX_BIN=bin/crabbox scripts/live-crownest-smoke.sh`. It exercised doctor, one-shot run, keep, status, reuse, and stop:

```text
released lease=[redacted] sandbox=[redacted]
classification=live_crownest_smoke_passed
```

## Config / secrets

- Crownest requires `CRABBOX_CROWNEST_API_KEY` or `CROWNEST_API_KEY` to be supplied at runtime.
- The API key is intentionally not persisted in Crabbox config and is not passed as a command-line argument.
- Provider docs cover the current delegated-run behavior and note that command environment forwarding is not supported yet.
target.author: tristanmanchester
